//: tt_um_fft  —  LibreLane config  (SKY130 HD, 6×2 tile)
CLOCK
100 ns = 10 MHz. Design has ~55 ns of positive setup slack,
meaning it could run ~3–4× faster. Tighten to 30 ns (33 MHz)
if you ever want to measure real Fmax — but 100 ns is safe for tapeout.
Run CTS on rst_n as well as clk.
rst_n drives 1018 FFs — without a CTS buffer tree it causes
2000+ slew violations in the ss corner. Treating it as a
second clock net gives it a balanced H-tree just like clk.
Remove 'rst_n' here if your SDC already constrains it differently.
SYNTHESIS
Limit fanout at synthesis stage so Yosys inserts buffer trees
for high-fanout combinational nets (e.g. enable signals).
16 matches the CTS max fanout to avoid conflicting targets.
PLACEMENT
57% utilisation in both runs — 60% density gives the placer
a little breathing room without risking congestion.
Enable resizer timing optimisations at placement stage.
Hold slack margins — the min_ff corner exits at 102 ps above
zero. Keep 0.15/0.10 to give the tool a comfortable buffer.
These were 0.1/0.05 before; the extra headroom prevents
marginal hold paths appearing after RCX parasitic extraction.
Slew and cap margins (%) — 20% padding above the library limit.
This tells the resizer to start buffering nets that are at
80% of the DRV limit, before they become actual violations.
Buffer primary input ports (e.g. ui_in[*]) — reduces input
transition time on long downstream paths.
CLOCK TREE SYNTHESIS
Cluster size of 8 sinks per CTS buffer — balanced for 1109 FFs.
Smaller = more balanced tree, bigger = fewer cells. 8 is a good
midpoint for this design's skew vs. area tradeoff.
Max fanout per CTS buffer. Consistent with SYNTH_MAX_FANOUT.
GLOBAL ROUTING / RESIZER
Enable resizer optimisations post-global-routing.
Hard fanout constraint enforced by OpenROAD on all nets.
10 is stricter than SYNTH_MAX_FANOUT (16) intentionally —
synthesis may not see all fanout after mapping, so PnR cleans up.
Wire length limit (µm) for the resizer buffer insertion pass.
── WHY 400: The tile core is ~1025 µm wide × 220 µm tall.
Setting this to 150 (previous attempt) fragmented nets into
dozens of short stubs, exploding cap violations from 2 → 48.
400 µm targets genuinely long cross-tile wires without
creating artificial intermediate-node cap problems.
── NOTE: MAX_TRANSITION_CONSTRAINT has been intentionally removed.
The previous value of 0.4 ns is below the SKY130 HD library's
natural slew range at the ss/100C/1v60 corner (~1.0–1.5 ns).
Setting it that low does not improve silicon — it just inflates
the reported violation count. Let the library defaults apply.
LINTING
!!! DO NOT CHANGE ANYTHING BELOW THIS LINE !!!
Skip KLayout DRC/XOR (Magic DRC is the TT signoff check)
Don't buffer outputs (TT does this at the top level)
Tile margin multipliers (TT 6×2 standard)
Absolute die sizing (TT provides the DEF template)
Allow global routing congestion (required for dense TT tiles)
I/O pin lengths
PDN vertical pitch (TT standard)
CTS is required
No power rings (TT block, not top-level chip)
Suppress Magic DEF label issues that break LVS
LEF export: pin geometry only, no connected net geometry
